body.title: Coherence inhibitor gates WINNOW-hint-dispatched LLM replies as habit output
body.result: main.py:6771-6789 — source_label for coherence inhibitor now gates 'habit:' on memory_type==PROCEDURAL. INTERPRETIVE winners (WINNOW_* hints) labeled 'llm_via_<id>' instead. suppress_incoherent's LLM-exemption already covers llm_via_ prefix, preserving the reply. 37 tests pass including new TestLlmViaLabel class + main.py source regression guard. Commit shipped — Igor needs restart.
body.description:
response_coherence_inhibitor.suppress_incoherent exempts `llm_or_tier0` but treats every `habit:*` source_label as potentially-templated. Today 2026-04-24T14:00:45 Igor generated a genuine conversational reply ('What's the thing you've been carrying around in your head lately...') in response to 'time for you to ask me a question. anything at all'; reply was 0-overlap on content tokens (Jaccard 0.000 < threshold 0.10) because the user prompt and the answer share no content words — normal conversational behavior. Inhibitor suppressed it. Reply was visible in console but never hit the channel, so web interface never saw it.

Root cause: main.py:6771-6772 constructs `_source_label` as `habit:<_turn_habit.id>` for ANY winning BG entry. WINNOW_xxx entries are INTERPRETIVE memories (winnowing hints) that drive LLM dispatch, not PROCEDURAL templated-response habits. Mislabeling them as `habit:` routes the LLM-produced reply through the suppression path.

Fix: only label as `habit:` when the winning memory is PROCEDURAL (genuine templated-response habit). For INTERPRETIVE/FACTUAL winners (WINNOW hints etc.), label as `llm_via_<id>` so suppress_incoherent's existing LLM-exemption rule covers them.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/main.py:6771-6772 (source_label construction), tests/test_response_coherence_inhibitor.py (new test), tests/test_coherence_inhibitor_llm_gate.py (may need parallel coverage).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — change label construction + test. OUT — redesigning the Jaccard coherence check, changing suppress_incoherent's contract.
**Test plan:** unit test: PROCEDURAL winner → label starts with `habit:`; INTERPRETIVE winner (simulating WINNOW_xxx) → label starts with `llm_via_`; regression test using today's observed prompt/response pair asserts suppression does NOT fire.
